intro: Thursday's big story is Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview — already finding thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities — as the company's revenue run rate hits $30B. Google drops Gemma 4 open-source, AI security startups raise hundreds of millions, and India's AI accountability push intensifies.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
top_stories.summary: Anthropic has unveiled Claude Mythos Preview, a cybersecurity-focused AI model deployed through 'Project Glasswing' with over 40 partners including Microsoft, Amazon, Apple, Google, and CrowdStrike. The model has already discovered thousands of previously unknown zero-day vulnerabilities across major systems — a landmark moment for AI in offensive and defensive security.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic Revenue Run Rate Hits $30B as Google and Broadcom Expand Compute Deal
top_stories.summary: Anthropic has significantly expanded its compute agreement with Google and Broadcom amid surging demand for Claude models. The company's annualised revenue has rocketed to $30 billion, with more than 1,000 business customers each spending over $1 million annually — signalling a rapid shift from AI experimentation to enterprise production.

top_stories.headline: Google Releases Gemma 4 — Open Model Built for Reasoning and Agentic Workflows
top_stories.summary: Google has launched Gemma 4, its latest open model series, released under the Apache 2.0 licence. Built specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, Gemma 4 continues Google's push to make capable open-weight models available to developers worldwide.

top_stories.headline: Rhoda AI Launches with $450M Series A and FutureVision Robotic Intelligence Platform
top_stories.summary: Rhoda AI has publicly launched with $450 million in Series A funding, unveiling FutureVision — a robotic intelligence system built on video-predictive control. The raise signals massive investor appetite for physical AI and embodied intelligence as the next frontier beyond language models.

top_stories.headline: White House AI Action Plan: 90+ Federal Policy Actions Across Innovation, Infrastructure, and Diplomacy
top_stories.summary: The White House has outlined over 90 federal policy actions spanning three pillars: accelerating AI innovation, building American AI infrastructure, and leading in international AI diplomacy and security. The plan marks the most comprehensive US federal AI policy directive to date and sets the tone for global regulatory conversations.

quick_hits.text: Trent AI emerges from stealth with $13M seed funding to secure AI agents and enterprise workflows against adversarial threats.
Cyberhill Partners raises $11M to bring AI to enterprise operations, targeting mid-market companies lacking in-house AI expertise.
Claude Mythos Preview scores 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified and 94.6% on GPQA Diamond — the highest published scores on both benchmarks.
Connecticut's General Assembly moves AI policy bills toward a vote, debating the balance between innovation headroom and protections for privacy and civil rights.
March 2026 saw over 30 new AI model releases — the highest monthly count ever recorded — from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and NVIDIA.

india_roundup.text: IndiaAI Innovation Challenge 2026 opens applications jointly with the Ministry of AYUSH and Ministry of MSME — winners get a two-year contract worth up to ₹1 crore to deploy AI in public health and MSME governance.
India has emerged as the world's third most competitive country in AI, with PM Modi's vision of democratising technology to address India-centric challenges driving the national roadmap.
Three out of four Indian companies now see AI as existential to their business, and nearly half already have multiple use cases in production — a sharp jump from 2025 figures.
India AI Impact Summit 2026 focuses on AI's impact on the creative economy — animation, gaming, and new storytelling formats — alongside regulation and the future of creative work.
New plan outlines how India will democratise AI infrastructure — targeting affordable compute access and open datasets for startups and researchers outside metro cities.

closing_insight: When an AI model is deployed specifically to find security vulnerabilities — and finds thousands within weeks — we have crossed from AI as productivity tool to AI as active participant in the infrastructure of trust. The implications for defence, regulation, and software supply chains will take years to fully surface.
